task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Tier 1 activities (Quiz and Page) in Section 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Drug Classification Exam' (Quiz) and 'Pharmacokinetics Reading' (Page) in Section 1 of the NUR401 course page. Screenshots of the quiz settings or edit page should show a 30-minute time limit, 2 attempts, a 75% passing grade, and 5 questions added from the 'Pharmacology Fundamentals' bank.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Tier 2 (Assignment) and Tier 3 (Forum) activities in their respective sections.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Medication Safety Case Study' (Assignment) in Section 2 and 'Clinical Drug Interaction Analysis' (Forum) in Section 3 of the course page. The forum settings should indicate it is a Q&A forum with 'Average of ratings' on a 1-5 scale.

task_completion.description: Verify the tiered access restrictions are applied to the Assignment and Forum.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the course homepage, look for 'Restricted' badges under the activities. The 'Medication Safety Case Study' should show it is restricted until the 'Drug Classification Exam' is passed AND the 'Pharmacokinetics Reading' is marked complete. The 'Clinical Drug Interaction Analysis' forum should show it is restricted until the 'Medication Safety Case Study' is passed.

task_completion.description: Verify the gradebook is configured with weighted categories and the activities are correctly placed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Gradebook setup' page. The aggregation method should be 'Weighted mean of grades' (or weights column explicitly visible and editable). There must be three categories: 'Foundation' (weight 25.0), 'Application' (weight 35.0), and 'Synthesis' (weight 40.0). The Quiz, Assignment, and Forum must be nested inside their respective categories.

task_completion.description: Verify the course completion criteria require the specific activities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 'Course completion' settings page. Under the 'Condition: Activity completion' section, the checkboxes for 'Drug Classification Exam', 'Medication Safety Case Study', and 'Clinical Drug Interaction Analysis' must be selected as required for course completion.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Moodle web interface to configure the course.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Moodle web interface in a browser, with standard Moodle navigation, forms, and buttons being clicked, rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the settings and restrictions are genuine Moodle UI elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The restriction labels, gradebook setup tables, and course completion forms should match the standard Moodle 4.5+ appearance, not mocked up text in a document.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots should show a logical progression of navigating through settings, filling out forms, and saving, rather than a sudden appearance of the final configured state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The course is NUR401 (Clinical Pharmacology). The agent must create 4 activities: 'Drug Classification Exam' (Quiz with 5 questions from 'Pharmacology Fundamentals'), 'Pharmacokinetics Reading' (Page), 'Medication Safety Case Study' (Assignment), and 'Clinical Drug Interaction Analysis' (Q&A Forum). The gradebook must use 'Weighted mean of grades' with categories Foundation (25%), Application (35%), and Synthesis (40%). Access restrictions must be visible on the course page (Assignment restricted by Quiz and Page; Forum restricted by Assignment). Course completion must require the Quiz, Assignment, and Forum.
